name: Soneja - Segorbe – Circular route via the Ojos Negros Greenway and the Salto de la Novia
name_original: Soneja - Segorbe – Circular por la Vía Verde de Ojos Negros y el Salto de la Novia
region_intro: Touring cycling around Soneja offers diverse terrain within the Alto Palancia region of Castellón, Spain. The area is characterized by its mountainous landscape, situated between the Natural Parks of Calderona and Sierra Espadán, and features the River Palancia. Cyclists can explore varied environments, including pine forests, cork oak groves, and the unique Laguna de la Dehesa. The region also provides access to the Ojos Negros Greenway, a significant long-distance cycling route. Best touring cycling routes around Soneja The most popular touring cycling route is Soneja - Segorbe – Circular route via the Ojos Negros Greenway and the Salto de la Novia, a 16.6 miles (26.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 1 minute to complete. This route follows sections of the Ojos Negros Greenway, offering a moderate cycling experience. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Rest Area at Arguinas Viaduct – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Estación de Soneja, a moderate 26.7 miles (42.9 km) path. This loop incorporates the Ojos Negros Greenway, passing by the Arguinas Viaduct. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Geldo and Segorbe – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Estación de Soneja, a 12.5 miles (20.2 km) trail leading through the Ojos Negros Greenway, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Touring cycling around Soneja is defined by mountainous terrain, the River Palancia, and its connection to the Ojos Negros Greenway. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Soneja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. More than 3000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Soneja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Soneja?
A: There are over 170 touring cycling routes recorded around Soneja on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's a path for every cyclist.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Soneja?
A: Yes, Soneja offers several easy touring cycling routes. While many routes incorporate the Ojos Negros Greenway, which is generally accessible, some are specifically designed for a more relaxed pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38291096" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Geldo and Segorbe – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Estación de Soneja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 20.2 km trail that can be completed in under two hours, offering scenic views along the greenway.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Soneja?
A: Soneja's touring cycling routes feature diverse terrain. You'll encounter mountainous landscapes, especially as the region sits between the Natural Parks of Calderona and Sierra Espadán. Routes often traverse pine forests, cork oak groves, and areas near the River Palancia. Many paths also utilize sections of the Ojos Negros Greenway, which offers a mix of paved and compacted gravel surfaces.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Soneja?
A: Yes, Soneja is a great starting point for circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934257836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soneja - Segorbe – Circular route via the Ojos Negros Greenway and the Salto de la Novia&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 26.8 km loop. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31495874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest Area at Arguinas Viaduct – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Estación de Soneja&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 42.9 km and offers varied scenery.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes in Soneja?
A: Soneja's routes offer access to several natural highlights. You can cycle near the unique Laguna de la Dehesa, a mountain lagoon recognized for its biodiversity. The area is also rich in water features, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1310997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bride's Leap Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2099448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tío Juan Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ajuez Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is another scenic spot you might encounter on longer routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38278307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Piscinas Bar – Ajuez Reservoir loop from Estación de Soneja&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest to explore while cycling in Soneja?
A: Absolutely. Soneja boasts a rich historical and hydraulic heritage. You can explore the Arabic layout of its historic center, visit the 18th-century San Miguel Arcángel Parish Church, or see the ruins of Soneja's 19th-century Carlist Walls. The area also features historical hydraulic sites like La Mirla Wash House, Noyanta Wash House, and El Arco, a 14th-15th century aqueduct, which can make for interesting stops on your cycling tour.

Q: What is the Ojos Negros Greenway and how does it relate to cycling in Soneja?
A: The Ojos Negros Greenway (Vía Verde de Ojos Negros) is a significant long-distance cycling route that connects Teruel with Sagunto. Soneja is strategically connected to this greenway, making it a popular feature of many local touring cycling routes. It provides cyclists with well-maintained sections, often paved or compacted gravel, allowing for enjoyable rides through varied landscapes and charming towns. Several routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31495874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rest Area at Arguinas Viaduct – Ojos Negros Greenway loop from Estación de Soneja&lt;/a&gt;, directly incorporate parts of this greenway.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about Soneja?
A: The touring cycling routes in Soneja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 160 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse mountainous terrain, the scenic beauty of the River Palancia, and the excellent access to the Ojos Negros Greenway, which offers a variety of cycling experiences.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Soneja?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Soneja offers several difficult routes with significant elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31503014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ford over the Palància river – Rustic stone bridge loop from Estación de Soneja&lt;/a&gt;, a 53.4 km route with over 1100 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Soneja?
A: Given Soneja's location in the Alto Palancia region, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for touring cycling. Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons can still be enjoyable. Winter offers cooler rides, especially through the pine and cork oak forests.

Q: Can I find places to eat or rest along the cycling routes in Soneja?
A: Soneja and its surrounding towns offer opportunities for rest and refreshments. The region is known for its rural tourism, and you'll find small villages and towns along many routes where you can stop for a break. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38278307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Piscinas Bar – Ajuez Reservoir loop from Estación de Soneja&lt;/a&gt;, even mention specific establishments, indicating that amenities are available in certain areas.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots recommended for touring cyclists in Soneja?
A: The region around Soneja is rich in scenic beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4047351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Dehesa viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers stunning views of the unique mountain lagoon. Many routes along the Ojos Negros Greenway also provide panoramic vistas of the surrounding valleys and mountains. The mountainous terrain itself ensures numerous picturesque spots throughout your ride.
tips:
One of the most recognized Greenways in all of Spain with a total length of more than 120 kilometers linking Teruel with Sagunto. Along it you can enjoy varied landscapes and pass through beautiful towns in the interior of Aragon and the Valencian Community. In terms of surface, you will find sections of pavement and others will be compacted gravel track. You will go through tunnels that require lights so remember to have them ready.
And as for the slope, from Teruel to Sagunto it is 90% downhill so you will be very comfortable, conversely you will have to overcome a slope of over 1000 metres.
No matter how you decide to go through it, you will still have a unique experience.
A legend surrounds this wonderful place with two waterfalls and many springs at least as dramatically as William Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet.

Segorbe is a very charming town, of great cultural, historical and scenic interest with very colorful and picturesque buildings. It is advisable to get lost in the streets and walk through it, since in every corner there is something curious or striking and it has many buildings of great historical importance such as the Cathedral.
